mnemonics: CRIME: Cross-Validation Results In Model Evaluation (to remember that cross-validation is a key technique for assessing model performance before the final exam)
keyPoints: Evaluation Metrics: Understand precision, recall, F1-score, ROC-AUC for classification; MSE, MAE, R-squared for regression.
Bias-Variance Tradeoff: High bias means underfitting, high variance means overfitting; regularization techniques help manage this.
Cross-Validation: Use k-fold cross-validation to get a robust estimate of model performance and avoid overfitting on the test set.
commonMistakes: Confusing precision and recall; precision is about false positives, recall is about false negatives.
Using the test set for model tuning or feature selection, which leads to data leakage and overly optimistic performance estimates.
Not considering the baseline model (e.g., predicting the most frequent class) as a comparison point for classification tasks.
